name: Herman Melville
abstract: Herman Melville (born Melvill; August 1, 1819 – September 28, 1891) was an American writer of the American Renaissance period. Among his best-known works are Moby-Dick (1851); Typee (1846), a romanticized account of his experiences in Polynesia; and Billy Budd, Sailor, a posthumously published novella.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: except maybe for Herman Melville or Hawthorne or people who thought in such deep and complex ways
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
